message: What's wrong, is something the matter?
What is it? Do I have something on my face?
You know that psychiatrist, Günter, right?
Yeah. I was getting counseling from him.
Well, I saw your file at Günter's.
If you have any worries, I'm here to help.
the only thing is that you've been ignoring me lately.
Please, I feel like my heart is going to burst. It hurts so much.
Come on, when I see you like that,
what's wrong? This isn't the first time, is it?
Did you start to dislike me?
while confirming my presence, he breathes short, soft gasps.
In contrast to his cold body,
that part feels warm, almost hot.
he breaths heavily, his beautiful face distorted in anguish.
Sweat lightly glistens on his exposed
body, and the sweet scent fills the room.
Your friend had misfortune, are you feeling better?
No, as long as you're okay, that's enough.
Though I should have spent a long time in this room, there’s hardly anything that can be called Jennifer's belongings.
there's no one else. It seems like guests rarely come.
It's a bland hotel room. No matter which piece of furniture or decoration you look at, you can't feel anyone’s preferences or feelings.
Her beautiful face, adorned with a slight smile just like always,
Knowing that I was close to Guy and the others, is she trying to comfort me today?
Her eyes are looking at me with even more affection than usual.
Despite suddenly asking, he doesn't look displeased. It almost feels like he's genuinely enjoying meeting me.
There's a rumor that Guy and Kristy are the culprits behind a serial murder case, but you shouldn't worry about it.
Why not? You can't deny that possibility, right?
What are you saying? You can't trust your friends?!
The situation speaks for itself that they are suspicious.
You're not in a good place right now. I think you shouldn't be like that.
What do you want me to do?
I want you to find the truth. You're a detective after all.
Since you're a detective, you should pursue the truth.
It's tough to have to doubt even close friends, isn't it?
The director is troubled about Patricia. He says raising his daughter alone is tough.
Especially since he's a busy man too.
That's true. But with things getting so complicated...
I wonder if it will be okay.
The director surprisingly seems like a doting dad. I feel like he's letting her get away with too much by always checking her mood.
Fathers say they're afraid of their daughters...
And the director is just like that.
What if the culprit is Guy or Kristy?
What do you mean, what if?
Can you handle it if your friends are the culprits?
I can't tell if they're the culprits or not. Of course, I hope they're not.
First of all, if they were the culprits, where are the severed parts? And what's the point of that?
Right, I remember now. The victim was dismembered.
It really is a case with too many mysteries.
Are Guy and the others really the culprits, or...
Sure, they don’t look very innocent.
But when they come looking for me, they're pretty bold, to the point where it takes me by surprise.
It seems like they're caring for me because of Guy and Kristy's misfortune. Their tone feels softer than usual.
I wonder how long they plan to stay in New York.
I understand your worries about Patricia, but
aren't you being a bit too anxious?
It's after the unexpected deaths of Guy and Kristy; I get the concern for their daughter. But I think Patricia has nothing to do with Guy and the others, right?
If Patricia doesn't come back soon, it seems like the director might break down first.
Since the deaths of Guy and Kristy, the police seem to think they might have been the perpetrators of a serial murder.
It's true that we can't prove Guy and the others are innocent,
but if I don't do something, they'll really pin the crime on me.
I hope this can all end with this incident.
If that happens, then it would definitely mean that Guy and the others are the culprits.
